archetype: Automated Iceberg Detection and Alert System for Arctic Shipping Routes
description: This system uses satellite and sensor data to detect and track icebergs in Arctic shipping routes, alerting vessels and maritime authorities to potential hazards. It is used by shipping companies, maritime authorities, and research institutions. The system must accurately detect and track icebergs in real-time, adhering to international maritime regulations and standards.
rules: MUST adhere to International Maritime Organization (IMO) regulations for Arctic shipping
MUST integrate with existing Automatic Identification System (AIS) infrastructure
MUST use satellite data from the European Space Agency's (ESA) Copernicus program
MUST incorporate data from ship-based radar and sensor systems
MUST provide alerts and warnings in accordance with the International Convention for the Safety of Life at Sea (SOLAS)
anti_patterns: DO NOT rely solely on satellite data, as it may not provide sufficient resolution in Arctic regions
DO NOT ignore ship-based sensor data, as it provides critical real-time information
DO NOT fail to account for sea ice drift and movement in iceberg tracking
key_technical_challenges: Accurate detection and tracking of icebergs in low-resolution satellite images
Integration of disparate data sources (satellite, radar, sensor) in real-time
Ensuring system reliability and uptime in harsh Arctic environments

recommended_stack.language: Python + its extensive libraries for satellite image processing and geospatial analysis
recommended_stack.framework: Django + its built-in support for real-time data processing and alerts
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostGIS + its spatial database capabilities for storing and querying iceberg locations
recommended_stack.cache: Redis + its ability to handle high-volume, low-latency data
recommended_stack.message_broker: RabbitMQ + its support for real-time messaging and alerts
